about: Non-circular relevance labels for the FortiOS ops-KB RAG eval (core/eval/fortios_corpus.py).
labeling_rule: For each real R230 FortiGate incident, a manual SECTION is labelled RELEVANT iff its prose
explains the MECHANISM behind that incident's documented root cause (the section a network
operator would open to understand or confirm the cause). Relevance was decided by READING the
section content, not by running any retriever. section_id values are the docs.fortinet.com
administration-guide numeric ids; a retrieval that surfaces any chunk of a relevant section
counts as retrieving that section (passage->document collapse).
why_noncircular: The invalidated IODA +334% defined relevance by the retriever's own entity+time join key, so
the 'structured' retriever trivially reconstructed it. Here the labels are the SEMANTIC match
between an operator's incident wording and a manual section's meaning. Every retriever in the
pipeline scores on query<->chunk TEXT (BM25 term overlap / bge cosine / cross-encoder pair
score); the labels use neither the query string nor any such score — they use the section's
root-cause meaning. There is no shared key (no join, no title match, no term-overlap threshold)
a retriever could reconstruct to earn the label, so a high score is only obtainable by genuine
query<->section semantic matching. Several relevant sections (e.g. 279661, 38214) barely share
surface terms with the incident query, which is why BM25 alone misses them.
query_note: query_fallback is authored here (operator phrasing) so the eval runs on a fresh checkout; when domains/network_rca/fixtures/real/heldout_cases.json is present the eval uses the REAL incident query text instead (never committed).

labels.query_fallback: FortiGate logged many failed administrator login attempts and then admin login was locked out; what is the root cause?

rationale.631730: System > Settings > 'Setting the administrator password retries and lockout time' — documents admin-lockout-threshold / admin-lockout-duration: after N failed admin password retries the admin is locked out. This IS the mechanism that disabled admin login after the brute-force flood.
rationale.279661: User & Authentication > 'Configuring the maximum log in attempts and lockout period' — 'Failed log in attempts can indicate malicious attempts...' and auth-lockout-threshold/duration. The manual's general treatment of failed-login-driven lockout behind this incident.

labels.query_fallback: A very high volume of flows from internal hosts is being denied by the FortiGate; what is the root cause?

rationale.656084: Policy and Objects > Policies > 'Firewall policy' — 'Any traffic going through a FortiGate has to be associated with a policy ... even whether or not it's allowed to pass.' Traffic matching no accept policy is denied (implicit deny). This is why internal-host flows are denied.
rationale.525166: Troubleshooting > 'Verifying the correct firewall policy is being used' — the runbook to confirm which policy is handling (and denying) the traffic via the policy Count column and packet-flow debug. This is how an operator confirms the deny is policy-expected.

labels.query_fallback: The FortiGate event log shows repeated session-clash entries; what is the root cause?

rationale.562859: Troubleshooting > 'Using a session table' — defines what a session is, the session table, and each session's entry (the 5-tuple/state). A 'session clash' is a session-table collision event; this is the manual content that explains sessions and the session table behind it. The admin guide has no literal 'session clash' page, so this is the semantically-correct section (and BM25 cannot reach it from the word 'clash').

labels.query_fallback: The FortiGate is emitting many DHCP ACK and statistics events; what is the state of DHCP address allocation?

rationale.783526: Network > 'DHCP servers and relays' — 'A DHCP server dynamically assigns IP addresses ... define up to ten address ranges ... lease time.' The mechanism of DHCP address allocation the ACK events reflect.
rationale.38214: Dashboards and Monitors > Network dashboard > 'DHCP monitor' — 'shows all the addresses leased out by FortiGate's DHCP servers ... revoke a lease'. This is the exact runbook for 'the state of DHCP address allocation' the incident asks about.

labels.query_fallback: FortiGuard updates are succeeding and security-rating summaries appear; what is the security posture?

rationale.292634: Fortinet Security Fabric > 'Security rating' — the Security Posture / Fabric Coverage / Optimization scorecards that summarize security posture. Directly the 'security-rating summary' in the incident.
rationale.783883: System > FortiGuard > 'Configuring FortiGuard updates' — the FortiGuard update mechanism whose success ('updates are succeeding') is part of the posture assessment.

labels.query_fallback: Many denied flows target camera/DVR service ports such as 37777 and 37809; what is the root cause?

rationale.656084: Policy and Objects > Policies > 'Firewall policy' — the probes to device service ports (e.g. Dahua 37777) match no accept policy and are denied/contained by the firewall policy. Shares this section with real_internal_deny_flood because both root causes ARE policy deny; this overlap is honest (the manual has no camera-port-specific page), not label inflation.
